text:
Please review the patch issue plan, no other plans should be read. Something is fundamentally wrong with either the way the tool is working or the way it's described how to use or something. Because even ChatGPT models are struggling to
use it and fail very often while using this tool, but the one in my reference in the crumbs package, it rarely ever fails using.

I know there are some structural differences because we tried to improve the tool, but there's obviously something fundamentally wrong between these two that needs to be worked out.

Built a new `patch` extension tool to replace the built in one. I'm seeing a lot of code here that does not meet the Lyle ethos. Single line functions, patterns that dont earn their keep. I dont want functionality to change at all, but is it possible to accomplish what we are with fewer lines of code?

I would like to create a similar tool to apply patch from this reference repo. I want it to work nearly the same as far as the outcome of the tool but I would like you to basically pitch the much better, cleaner version of code that we
should implement. I also do not
I would rather do a real repo. This repo has barely anything in it. Pi is considered one of the best repos

Okay so this is going to be a big one for us to really plan thoroughly and come up with a good design and architecture. To be honest, I only have rough idea of what I want to do here, so before we even get into implementation
architecture, I want you to help me figure out and potentially even run tests on what might be best.
